title: Reading Workshop on Digital Competition & Regulation
text:
Workshop Programme
    Please enter the password to view the programme.
    
    Enter
    Incorrect password. Please try again.
  






Day 1: Friday, June 12, 2026Day 2: Saturday, June 13, 2026




08:30 – 08:55



☕


Coffee and Registration







08:55 – 09:00



🗣️


Welcome







09:00 – 10:30



Session 1: Information, AI, and Platform Quality




Antonio Russo (IMT Business School) 09:00 – 09:30


AI Summaries and Content Discovery




Wynne Lam (University of Edinburgh) 09:30 – 10:00


Innovation and Platform Information Disclosure




Leonardo Madio (University of Padova) 10:00 – 10:30


Curation with moral hazard: Why platforms host low quality







10:30 – 11:00



☕


Coffee Break







11:00 – 12:00



Session 2: Sectoral Platforms and Price Matching




Rhys Williams (University of Cambridge) 11:00 – 11:30


Never Knowingly Undersold: Do Price Match Guarantees Result in Lower Prices?




Anna Rita Bennato (Loughborough University) 11:30 – 12:00


Local Energy for Sale: Pricing and Participation on Energy Trading Platform







12:00 – 13:30



🍽️


Lunch Break


Blandfords, Park House







13:30 – 13:45



Policy Keynote




Lewis McQuarrie (Digital Regulation Cooperation Forum)







13:45 – 15:15



Session 3: Open Banking, Smart Data, and Price Discrimination




Helen Weeds (Charles River Associates and Centre for Competition Policy) 13:45 – 14:15


Open Banking Payments: Promoting Competition and Innovation in Retail Payments




Yiquan Gu (University of Reading) 14:15 – 14:45


UK’s Smart Data Schemes


When Data Calls the Tune: Price Personalization and Price Leadership




Ioana Chioveanu (Nottingham University Business School) 14:45 – 15:15


Price Discrimination Under Asymmetric Access to Consumer Data







15:15 – 15:45



☕


Coffee Break







15:45 – 17:15



Session 4: Retail Coordination, Platform Services, and Encroachment




Nicolas de Roos (University of Liverpool) 15:45 – 16:15


Negotiating Coordination: A Study in Retail Gasoline




Shiva Shekhar (Tilburg University) 16:15 – 16:45


Data Regulation, Platform Encroachment, and the Matthew Effect




Greg Taylor (University of Oxford) 16:45 – 17:15


Seller-Side Tying of Platform Services







17:30



🥤


Drinks Reception


Park House







19:00



🍽️


Workshop Dinner


Côte Reading · Dinner Pre-order









08:30 – 09:00



☕


Coffee and Registration







09:00 – 10:30



Session 5: Data infrastructure, Health, and Privacy




Luca Sandrini (University of Padova and ZEW Mannheim) 09:00 – 09:30


Agglomeration and Strategic Congestion in Data Centers




Burkhard Hehenkamp (Paderborn University) 09:30 – 10:00


Sustainable hospital regulation: On the welfare implications of digital treatments




Stefano Colombo (Università Cattolica del Sacro Cuore) 10:00 – 10:30


Strategic anonymity and behavior-based pricing







10:30 – 11:00



☕


Coffee Break







11:00 – 12:00



Keynote




Andrew Rhodes (TSE) 11:00 – 12:00


Digital Ecosystems and Data Regulation







12:00 – 13:15



🍽️


Lunch Break


HBS Foyer







13:15 – 14:45



Session 6: Algorithms, Steering, and Data Markets




Subhasish Modak Chowdhury (University of Sheffield) 13:15 – 13:45


Default Search Engine Settings and Consumer Switch Behaviour: Results from a Field Experiment




Muxin Li (Fudan University) 13:45 – 14:15


Demand Steering and Market Selection: Vertical Contracts and Algorithmic Allocation




Tobias Wenzel (DICE) 14:15 – 14:45


Mandating privacy spillovers: Interoperability in data markets







14:45



🗣️


Concluding Thanks







15:00



🥤


Post-workshop Drinks


Park House

title: CV and Contact
text:
2022 - : Professor of Spatial Economics and Industrial Organization, Henley Business School, University of Reading
2011 - 2022: Lecturer (2011-15), Senior Lecturer (2015-19) and Reader (2019-2022) in Economics, University of Liverpool Management School

2020 - 2022: Institutional Lead for the North West Social Science Doctoral Training Partnership

2006 - 2011: Wissenschaftlicher Mitarbeiter in Forschung und Lehre, TU Dortmund University

title: CV and Contact
text:
Education

BSc Management, Tongji University

title: Yiquan Gu
text:
Professor of Spatial Economics and Industrial Organization at Henley Business School, University of Reading
British Academy and Bridging Responsible AI Divides Innovation Fellow at the Digital Regulation Cooperation Forum
Associate Editor, Economic Modelling
Main research interests: Industrial Organization, Digital Economy and Behavioural Economics


Recent Publications

Product differentiation, economies of scale and entry (with Tobias Wenzel), Ungated Working Paper 📝, Economics Letters, forthcoming
Firm Prominence and Price Framing (with Ioana Chioveanu and Tobias Wenzel), Journal of Economic Behavior and Organization, March 2026
Park Life: the difference between having financial information and understanding it on park home owners’ quality of life (with Alexander Lord, Richard Dunning, Ian Wilson and Thomas Moore), Ungated Working Paper 📝, Town Planning Review, December 2025



Working Papers

Exclusive data, price manipulation and market leadership (with Leo Madio and Carlo Reggiani)
